Now, being a cleaner here locally but self-employed means I have to be careful with my health too—I can't afford to fall ill and lose income. So when people talk about vaccines online or in person now, it's something close to me personally.

I think the best way is to start by listening actively without interrupting everyone else’s point of view first because that shows respect for others’ opinions even if they differ greatly from mine own ones sometimes do on social media topics anyway. Then share your personal experience in a calm manner using simple words since my education level isn't too high but still I try hard to communicate clearly about things like cooking recipes or plant care.

My good mental health is crucial too because these discussions can get heated quickly especially online where you spend few times weekly maybe just scrolling through news about vaccines.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
Managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines can be a sensitive topic, especially for me as someone who grew up in Naples. I remember when my mother was getting her PhD from another city; she had to travel frequently because of work. That experience taught us children how important it is to take care of ourselves properly so that we don't get sick or miss out on opportunities like traveling again.

Now, being a cleaner here locally but self-employed means I have to be careful with my health too—I can't afford to fall ill and lose income. So when people talk about vaccines online or in person now, it's something close to me personally.

I think the best way is to start by listening actively without interrupting everyone else’s point of view first because that shows respect for others’ opinions even if they differ greatly from mine own ones sometimes do on social media topics anyway. Then share your personal experience in a calm manner using simple words since my education level isn't too high but still I try hard to communicate clearly about things like cooking recipes or plant care.

Also, it helps not just me but many others if we focus more on facts rather than emotions—after all, my parents are highly educated one with PhD studies while the other only went primary school so they understand better how science works sometimes. My good mental health is crucial too because these discussions can get heated quickly especially online where you spend few times weekly maybe just scrolling through news about vaccines.

And yes, following Jesus in Christianity helps me stay humble during disagreements—agreeableness score high might I say? That allows me to suggest improvements without sounding aggressive towards those who disagree with me on certain aspects of the conversation flow itself.
